# 14 — Await helpers: mailbox pollution, hidden polling, and raising streams

**Severity:** Low
**Location:** `lib/jido/await.ex` (`all/3`, `any/3`, `child/4`), `lib/jido/agent_server.ex` (`stream_status/2`)

## Problem

1. **Mailbox pollution in `all/3` / `any/3`.** Waiter processes `send(caller, {:await_result, ...})`
   directly to the caller. When the function returns early (first error, `any/2` winner,
   timeout), other waiters may have *already sent* their results — killing them doesn't
   recall messages in flight. Those `{:await_result, ref, ...}` tuples stay in the
   caller's mailbox forever. Call `Jido.await_any/2` from inside a GenServer (a
   coordinator agent is the obvious place) and you'll later receive unexplained info
   messages — or worse, a future `await_any` invocation in the same process could in
   principle be confused if refs weren't unique (they are, thanks to `make_ref()`, but
   the garbage still accumulates). A drain loop (`receive` with `after 0`) on the known
   ref set before returning would fix it; `Task.async_stream` or monitored tasks with
   `Task.shutdown` semantics would fix it idiomatically.

2. **`child/4` polls despite the no-polling pitch.** The module and `completion/3` docs
   emphasize event-driven waiting ("No polling is involved"), but `Await.child/4` busy-
   polls the parent's **full state** (finding 08) every 50ms until the tag appears. A
   parent that never spawns the tag costs 20 full-state copies per second until timeout.
   An event-driven alternative exists in-house: register a waiter for a
   `child.started`-style condition the way `await_completion` registers completion
   waiters.

3. **`stream_status/2` sleeps and raises.** `Stream.repeatedly` with `Process.sleep`
   inside means the sleep happens *before yielding each element* (including after the
   final element a consumer takes), and any lookup error raises a bare `RuntimeError`
   string out of the stream — surprising inside `Enum.reduce_while` pipelines. Returning
   `{:error, reason}` as a terminal element, or halting the stream, would compose better.

4. **`alive?/1` does a `GenServer.call`** (full state fetch) to answer a boolean; while
   the agent is busy (finding 03) this reports agents as not alive. `Process.alive?` on
   the resolved pid is both cheaper and more truthful to the name.

## Recommendation

Rebuild `all/3`/`any/3` on `Task.Supervisor.async_nolink` + `Task.yield_many/2`, make
`child/4` event-driven via a parent-side waiter, fix `alive?/1` to check process
liveness, and make `stream_status/2` halt-on-error. None of these change public return
shapes except the stream error case.
title: F14: Await helpers: mailbox pollution, hidden polling, and raising streams
